instruction: how many accounts did I blacklist in telegram? Answer only with the number.

steps.result_preview:
cat accounts/README.MD
Put account records into files `ACCOUNT_ID.json`.

Example filename:

```text
acct_001.json
```

Use JSON like this:

```json
{
  "id": "acct_001",
  "name": "Nordlicht Health",
  "legal_name": "Nordlicht Health GmbH",
  "industry": "healthcare",
  "region": "DACH",
  "country": "Germany",
  "tier": "strategic",
  "status": "active",
  "primary_contact_id": "cont_001",
  "account_manager": "John Novak",
  "last_contacted_on": "2026-03-02",
  "next_follow_up_on": "2026-03-1

acme-logistics.md
acme-robotics.md
aperture-ai-labs.md
blue-harbor-bank.md
canalport-shipping.md
greengrid-energy.md
helios-tax-group.md
nordlicht-health.md
northstar-forecasting.md
README.MD
silverline-retail.md

cat 01_notes/acme-logistics.md
# Acme Logistics

## Journal

Acme Logistics was a separate procurement path from Acme Robotics, driven by warehouse exception handling and cross-dock coordination pain in Benelux. The early deal was modest on purpose and sold as an operations experiment, which means the buying group still thinks in terms of proof and practical value rather than strategic platform language.

Because of the name overlap, this account is easy to conflate with the robotics one if some

cat 01_notes/acme-robotics.md
# Acme Robotics

## Journal

Acme Robotics came in through a systems integrator intro and responded well to a practical workflow audit instead of a broad product pitch. The underlying pain is the handoff between QA and plant operations, and they tend to engage when the conversation stays anchored in lost time, defect follow-up, and operational drift.

Important to remember that this is one of the two Acme accounts in the repo. The name collision is intentional, so a

cat 01_notes/aperture-ai-labs.md
# Aperture AI Labs

## Journal

Aperture AI Labs bought the AI insights add-on after the core workflow rollout had already earned trust with operations. That is useful context because the commercial relationship is not fragile, but the add-on still opened a separate security review path and should be treated as a distinct approval track.

This is a good account for adjacent expansion, as long as the conversation stays grounded in the existing rollout and does not

cat 01_notes/blue-harbor-bank.md
# Blue Harbor Bank

## Journal

Blue Harbor Bank started from a compliance-led pilot, so the real buyer energy has always been balanced by security, legal, and architecture caution. They reserved budget because the internal service request use case was credible, but every next step still routes through formal review.

This is not the kind of account where optimistic language helps. Better to stay conservative, name the review gates clearly, and avoid implying tha

cat 01_notes/canalport-shipping.md
# CanalPort Shipping

## Journal

CanalPort Shipping came from a port operations referral and the value case was obvious because the team was already drowning in manual exception tracking. The deal closed when the rollout was narrowed into something operationally safe instead of trying to fix every workflow at once.

The pain is real, but urgency stays medium because they can still absorb a lot through overtime and informal heroics. That means progress usually

cat 01_notes/greengrid-energy.md
# GreenGrid Energy

## Journal

GreenGrid Energy came through an advisor introduction and the early traction came from field operations pain, not from top-down strategy. The team saw enough waste in site-level issue follow-up to approve an initial subscription before budget windows closed, which is usually a good sign that the problem is tangible.
